description: You are a space weather analyst. On Feb 4, 2022, a severe geomagnetic storm caused the loss of 38 Starlink satellites because increased atmospheric density dragged them down before they could raise their orbits. You must simulate a similar scenario for a VLEO satellite to assess its survival time. Using the spacecraft parameters (Mass: 260 kg, Drag Area: 10 m^2, Cd: 2.2) and initial orbital state (260 km circular orbit) found in ~/Desktop/space_weather_brief_SL47.txt, create a GMAT script that simulates orbital decay using the JacchiaRoberts atmosphere model. Compare two scenarios: Nominal Conditions (F10.7=120, F10.7A=120, Kp/MagneticIndex=3) and Storm Conditions (F10.7=140, F10.7A=120, Kp/MagneticIndex=8). Propagate both until the altitude drops below 120 km or 15 days have elapsed. Save the GMAT script to ~/GMAT_output/storm_decay_simulation.script and write a summary report to ~/GMAT_output/storm_survival_report.txt including the survival days and final altitude (km) for both scenarios using the keys: nominal_survival_days, storm_survival_days, nominal_final_alt_km, and storm_final_alt_km. Login: username=ga, password=password123
